title: CryoNet.Refine: A One-step Diffusion Model for Rapid Refinement of Structural Models with Cryo-EM Density Map Restraints
summary: High-resolution structure determination by cryo-electron microscopy (cryo-EM) requires the accurate fitting of an atomic model into an experimental density map. Traditional refinement pipelines such as Phenix.real_space_refine and Rosetta are computationally expensive, demand extensive manual tuning, and present a significant bottleneck for researchers. We present CryoNet.Refine, an end-to-end deep learning framework that automates and accelerates molecular structure refinement. Our approach utilizes a one-step diffusion model that integrates a density-aware loss function with robust stereochemical restraints, enabling rapid optimization of a structure against experimental data. CryoNet.Refine provides a unified and versatile solution capable of refining protein complexes as well as DNA/RNA-protein complexes. In benchmarks against Phenix.real_space_refine, CryoNet.Refine consistently achieves substantial improvements in both model-map correlation and overall geometric quality metrics. By offering a scalable, automated, and powerful alternative, CryoNet.Refine aims to serve as an essential tool for next-generation cryo-EM structure refinement. Web server: https://cryonet.ai/refine; Source code: https://github.com/kuixu/cryonet.refine.

AI.tldr: CryoNet.Refine是一个端到端的深度学习框架，利用单步扩散模型加速和自动化冷冻电镜（cryo-EM）结构的原子模型精修，显著提升了模型质量和效率。
AI.motivation: 传统的cryo-EM结构精修流程计算成本高昂，需要大量手动调整，成为研究瓶颈。为了解决这个问题，需要一种更快速、自动化且强大的替代方案。
AI.method: CryoNet.Refine采用单步扩散模型，结合密度感知损失函数和稳健的立体化学约束，对结构进行优化，使其与实验数据更好地拟合。该框架适用于蛋白质复合物以及DNA/RNA-蛋白质复合物的精修。
AI.result: 在与Phenix.real space refine的基准测试中，CryoNet.Refine在模型-密度相关性和整体模型几何指标方面均有显著提升。
AI.conclusion: CryoNet.Refine有望成为下一代cryo-EM结构精修的重要工具，提供了一种可扩展、自动化且强大的替代方案，加速结构生物学研究。

title: Induction Meets Biology: Mechanisms of Repeat Detection in Protein Language Models
summary: Protein sequences are abundant in repeating segments, both as exact copies and as approximate segments with mutations. These repeats are important for protein structure and function, motivating decades of algorithmic work on repeat identification. Recent work has shown that protein language models (PLMs) identify repeats, by examining their behavior in masked-token prediction. To elucidate their internal mechanisms, we investigate how PLMs detect both exact and approximate repeats. We find that the mechanism for approximate repeats functionally subsumes that of exact repeats. We then characterize this mechanism, revealing two main stages: PLMs first build feature representations using both general positional attention heads and biologically specialized components, such as neurons that encode amino-acid similarity. Then, induction heads attend to aligned tokens across repeated segments, promoting the correct answer. Our results reveal how PLMs solve this biological task by combining language-based pattern matching with specialized biological knowledge, thereby establishing a basis for studying more complex evolutionary processes in PLMs.

AI.tldr: 本文研究了蛋白质语言模型（PLM）如何检测蛋白质序列中的重复片段，无论是完全相同的还是近似的。研究发现PLM的近似重复检测机制可以涵盖完全重复检测，并揭示了其内部运作机制。
AI.motivation: 蛋白质序列中的重复片段对于蛋白质结构和功能至关重要。虽然已知的算法可以识别重复片段，但蛋白质语言模型（PLM）在识别重复模式方面的能力尚未得到充分理解。本文旨在阐明PLM识别重复片段的内部机制。
AI.method: 研究人员通过逆向工程PLM的内部机制，识别了用于预测掩码token的电路，并分析了这些电路在处理完全重复和近似重复时的行为。他们深入研究了电路中的注意力头和多层感知器（MLP）神经元，以揭示其功能。
AI.result: 研究发现，PLM的近似重复检测机制可以涵盖完全重复检测。PLM的重复检测机制包含两个主要阶段：首先，模型构建特征表示，利用通用位置注意力头和生物特化的组件（如编码氨基酸相似性的神经元）；然后，归纳注意力头关注重复片段中的对齐token，从而促进正确的答案预测。
AI.conclusion: 本文揭示了PLM如何结合基于语言的模式匹配和专门的生物学知识来解决生物学任务，为研究PLM中更复杂的进化过程奠定了基础。这有助于改进重复检测流程，并提高对PLM预测的信心。
